coaching.title: Easy 5 mi — Recovery Week
coaching.message: Build Phase, Week 9 of 22, and this is a recovery week — so the only job today is an easy 5 miles in Zone 2 (cap it around 132-145 bpm). It should feel conversational the whole way; if you can't speak in full sentences, you're going too hard. You're at altitude in Antigua (~1,500m), so expect HR to drift 5-10 bpm higher than at sea level — run by effort, not pace, and back off if it creeps into Zone 3. No vert hunting today; save the climbing legs for the next quality block.

coaching.title: Fuel Right
coaching.message: Five easy miles doesn't need heavy fueling — a light carb top-off (banana or toast, ~30g) beforehand is plenty, and you can run it fasted if that's your norm. Hydrate well in the Guatemalan heat: 500ml with electrolytes before you head out. Afterward, hit the 30-45 min window with ~20-30g protein plus carbs to support the recovery-week adaptation. Keep total carbs near your ~220g rest-baseline today since volume is low.

coaching.title: Recovery
coaching.message: This is a recovery week — the easy run is the hard part, everything else should be restorative. Prioritize 8 hours of sleep, do 10 minutes of mobility/foam rolling on calves and hips, and keep your scheduled gym session light (mobility and activation, not heavy loading). Resist the urge to add miles; the whole point of this week is to let fitness consolidate before the next build push.

coaching.title: Mental Edge
coaching.message: 92 days to the Julian Alps 50K. A recovery week isn't a step back — it's how you absorb the work and show up stronger for the next block. Discipline this week looks like restraint, not effort. Practice the patience now that you'll need on race day when you hold back early on those alpine climbs so you have legs left for the back half.
